business.headline: Retaining users is really hard: Nithin Kamath on broking business' biggest challengeKamath said one of the biggest hurdles for stockbrokers is user retention. While many people open trading accounts, not all continue investing or trading for years.

Why Tier-II and Tier-III cities are driving India's next consumption boomFor years, India's consumer story centred on the metros. Today, smaller cities are rapidly taking the lead in spending, aspirations and premium consumption. Why are Tier-II and Tier-III cities booming?

India may open skies to new airlines in bid to challenge IndiGo-Air India duopolyIndia is considering easing rules to let airport operators such as Adani and GMR run airlines. The move is aimed at widening competition in a domestic market dominated by IndiGo and Air India.

Jana Nayagan's title card isn't a tribute to CM Vijay. It's a cheap shot at Stalin Jana Nayagan's title card gave Vijay's fans exactly what they wanted: a sitting Chief Minister as the hero. But burying an old, borrowed swipe at Stalin inside it turned a well-earned victory lap into an unnecessary, cheap parting shot.

Jana Nayagan X review: Vijay shines in an average film, say fans Jana Nayagan opened in theatres on Wednesday after a seven-month delay, with early X reviews split on Vijay's final film. The response highlights strong fan interest in his swansong even as viewers debate its story, length and second-half execution.

It wasn't my final cut: Anurag Kashyap says criticism of Bandar was 'quite valid' Anurag Kashyap said on Kunal Kamra's channel that he could not own the released cut of Bandar. He said studio edits changed the film's perspective, making the criticism against it valid.

Imran Khan joins student protest march in Mumbai, clicks selfies. Watch Imran Khan joined a Mumbai protest march backing the CJP-led student agitation after police action in Delhi. The demonstrations gathered wider celebrity support as the Centre moved to tighten security in the capital.

Huma Qureshi, Saqib Saleem visit Jantar Mantar late at night to back CJP protest Actors Huma Qureshi and Saqib Saleem visited Jantar Mantar late at night to show their support for the ongoing student protest against NEET examination irregularities. Is 2026 set to be the biggest year of Yash's career? Actor Yash is set to headline Toxic and Ramayana after his lengthy post-KGF gap. The back-to-back pan-India projects have put his range and box office pull under fresh focus.

Paper leak a serious issue, students showed courage: Salman Khan backs CJP protest Salman Khan has expressed his support for the CJP protest, commending students for their peaceful demand for educational reforms while cautioning against politicisation.

Aamir Khan's ex-sister-in-law says actor's brother Hyder Ali Khan has schizophrenia Eva Grover has spoken about her marriage to Hyder Ali Khan, alleging years of physical abuse amid his schizophrenia. She said the relationship ended in divorce, a long custody separation from her daughter and lasting concern over his present condition.

The End of Oak Street trailer: Anne Hathaway's suburb turns dinosaur battleground Warner Bros has released a new trailer and posters for The End of Oak Street, starring Anne Hathaway and Ewan McGregor. The footage stresses the film's core hook as the Platt family battles dinosaurs and tries to stay together after a cosmic event upends their neighbourhood.

Shraddha Kapoor hunts for new lead as Aamir Khan steps out of Ashneer Grover biopic Actor Aamir Khan has reportedly exited the Ashneer Grover biopic after creative differences with Rahul Mody. The film also featured actor-turned-producer Shraddha Kapoor in a key role.

general news.headline: Live: Traders' body urges CP offices, eateries to shut by 6:30 pm amid protestsCJP Protest in Delhi Live Updates: Delhi remained on edge as the NEET paper leak protests continued, with the Centre renewing its offer for talks even as political, legal and business concerns mounted. Union Minister Jitendra Singh urged protesting students to meet the government at any venue of their choice, saying both he and Health Minister JP Nadda were ready for discussions, but the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) insisted the talks be held at the protest site or a neutral location. Amid the prolonged agitation, the Chamber of Trade and Industry urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to designate a permanent protest venue outside central Delhi, claiming businesses had suffered nearly 70% losses due to metro station closures and traffic disruptions, while the New Delhi Traders Association advised establishments in Connaught Place to shut early. Political reactions also intensified, with Priyanka Gandhi questioning the police action against protesters and BJP MP Tejaswi Surya reiterating the government's commitment to education reforms and fast-track courts for paper leak cases. Meanwhile, a Delhi Police ACP was injured in stone-pelting, CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke briefly stepped away from the protest due to illness, and Sonam Wangchuk's wife Gitanjali Angmo defended the students, insisting their demonstration had remained peaceful. Stay with Indiatoday.in for the latest updates.

CJI vows intervention if Supreme Court metro station stays shut amid CJP protestThe Chief Justice said the Supreme Court administration had already been asked to take up the matter with the Delhi Metro authorities and assured the Bar that if the issue remained unresolved by lunchtime, he would personally intervene.

Congress's protest near PM's house insincere: Big remark by Wangchuk's wifeGitanjali Angmo, in an interview with India Today's Rajdeep Sardesai, criticised Rahul Gandhi's flash protest near Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence and said leaders should have joined students at Jantar Mantar instead.

Video shows Mumbai cop threatening to frame student protesters in drugs caseA video, posted by the Mumbai Congress and the Cockroach Party, shows a police driver threatening a group of student protesters with a false drug case if they returned to agitate. As it drew backlash, Mumbai Police ordered an inquiry into the incident.

I found out why thousands are flooding Jantar Mantar now. It's not Wangchuk or DipkeA protest once confined to a few thousand people has now swelled into a crowd too large to count accurately. Protesters say the July 20 lathi-charge and use of tear gas by Delhi Police and RAF personnel has transformed the frustration of students into anger, triggering a fresh wave of agitation at Jantar Mantar.

Bumper monsoon: 10,000-km-long rain cloud seen from India to South KoreaSatellite imagery has captured a vast monsoon rain band stretching from north India to South Korea. The spell has sharply lifted rainfall across India while prompting flood and landslide warnings in vulnerable regions.

The clarification came after reports and political criticism claimed the order was meant to curb dissent over the NEET paper leak.

How Akhilesh Yadav is using three stages in Delhi for one big gainFrom the CJP's Jantar Mantar platform shunned by ally Congress to Rahul Gandhi's 7 LKM protest to Parliament, Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav is using all stages in Delhi. The goal seems to be one, coming just ahead of the Assembly election in Uttar Pradesh.

sports.headline: Commonwealth Games 2026 Opening Ceremony: Timing, TV Channel, Live Streaming The Commonwealth Games 2026 open in Glasgow with a star-studded ceremony at the OVO Hydro. The event launches 11 days of competition and puts India in focus with Mirabai Chanu leading the contingent.

Keep faith in government: Dhawan urges patience from students amid protests Shikhar Dhawan asked protesting students to stay patient and trust the government amid the CJP protests in Delhi. Argentina fans launch petition to replay the World Cup 2026 final. Here's why Argentina supporters have launched a petition asking FIFA to replay the 2026 World Cup final after the loss to Spain. The campaign attacks the officiating of referee Slavko Vincic, calling it biased.

Ramos takes a jibe at Messi by recreating Argentina star's iconic World Cup pic After Spain beat Argentina in the 2026 World Cup final, Sergio Ramos recreated Lionel Messi's viral trophy-sleeping photo to take a light-hearted jibe at the Argentina captain. Messi and Co. were beaten by Spin 1-0 in the showdown in New York.

Rodri set for surgery after Spain's World Cup win as Real Madrid links persist Spain's World Cup-winning captain Rodri is set to undergo back surgery after a stellar tournament that earned him the Golden Ball. Messi's Inter Miami faces MLS investigation over alleged Casemiro deal tampering Inter Miami's signing of Casemiro has quickly come under scrutiny after MLS launched a tampering investigation into the transfer. Rahmat Shah to lead Afghanistan in Tests and ODIs, Gurbaz becomes vice-captainThe Afghanistan Cricket Board (ACB) has appointed experienced batter Rahmat Shah as the new captain of its Test and One-Day International (ODI) teams following the resignation of Hashmatullah Shahidi.

IPL founder Lalit Modi set to return to India after 16 yearsIPL founder and former chairman Lalit Modi has said he will return to India after 16 years following a tribunal verdict that gave him major relief in the 2009 IPL South Africa Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) case.

Sindhu wildly celebrates win vs Unnati Hooda, shares cryptic social media postChina Open 2026: A huge roar on court, followed by a cryptic message off it. PV Sindhu battled past Unnati Hooda in three games before sharing a social media post that sparked plenty of intrigue.

UTT: Kolkata edge U Mumba in thriller as both teams seal semis spotKolkata Thunder Blades ended defending champions U Mumba TT's winning streak with a dramatic 8-7 victory to secure a place in the UTT Season 7 semi-finals, with both teams advancing to the knockout stage.

Benched in England, Kuldeep Yadav signs for Yorkshire for one-dayers and CountyKuldeep Yadav has joined Yorkshire after being left out of India's ODI XI in England. The stint gives him immediate match time before the Sri Lanka Tests and a county run with the English club later in the year.

Judge us after: Fulton, tactical discipline, and India's quest to exorcise 1975For Craig Fulton, the aim at the World Cup is to end a 51-year-old semi-final curse for the Indian team. ICC ODI rankings: Rohit Sharma closes on Virat Kohli as No. 1 battle heats upRohit Sharma gained 14 rating points after his hundred at Lord's on Saturday. Three of the top four in the ICC rankings for men's ODIs are Indians. Shubman Gill is closing on in No. 1 Daryl Mitchell.

Ben Stokes battles cramps, scores first hundred after international retirementBen Stokes returned for Durham and made an unbeaten 108 against Derbyshire despite severe cramps. The innings underlined his resilience and gave Durham a five-wicket win days after his Test retirement call.

Embracing the rollercoaster, Sooryavanshi returns to favourite hunting groundZimbabwe vs India:Vaibhav Sooryavanshi returns to Harare for India's Zimbabwe T20Is after a testing debut in England. The 15-year-old hopes familiar conditions help convert promise into a first senior impact.

Scaloni rejects unfair refereeing chat: Spain were better in World Cup finalLionel Scaloni rejected complaints about the refereeing after Argentina's World Cup final defeat to Spain. He said Spain were the better side, while admitting that fatigue may have also been a reason behind the loss.

Hasmatullah Shahidi steps down as Afghanistan ODI captain historic 4-year runHashmatullah Shahidi has stepped down as Afghanistan's ODI captain but remains available for selection. His exit closes a notable phase that included a near semi-final World Cup run in 2023 and opens the search for a new skipper before the Ireland series.

David Warner pleads guilty to drink driving, faces up to 9 months in jailDavid Warner pleaded guilty to a mid-range drink-driving charge in Sydney through his lawyer. The conviction could bring jail time and trigger a review of his Sydney Thunder captaincy.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".
